title: Maxillaria dunstervilleorum
taxonomy: Maxillaria dunstervilleorum is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a member of the genus Maxillaria. The classification of Maxillaria dunstervilleorum is further broken down into the subfamily Spiranthoideae and tribe Maxillarieae. Maxillaria dunstervilleorum is native to the tropical regions of Central America. Its classification is an important aspect in understanding the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other species.

intro: Maxillaria dunstervilleorum, also known as the Dunsterville's Maxillaria, is a species of orchid native to Venezuela. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Maxillaria dunstervilleorum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, known as a "tisane," which is a popular herbal remedy among locals.
